Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user has provided a detailed prompt asking me to interpret a DECLARE model of a loan application process They want me to 1 Describe the loan approval process step by step based on the DECLARE constraints 2 Explain how each constraint ensures a compliant logically ordered process 3 Discuss the real world motivations behind these rules 4 Speculate on consequences if activities occurred out of order or were omitted

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me analyze the DECLARE constraints and construct a coherent narrative

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: INIT constraint Receive Application must be the first activity

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: EXISTENCE constraints Receive Application must occur at least once Gather Additional Documents must occur at least once

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: ABSENCE constraint Proceed Without Compliance must never occur

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: EXACTLY ONE constraint Preliminary Credit Check must occur exactly once

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: RESPONSE constraint Receive Application Preliminary Credit Check if A occurs B must occur

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SUCCESSION constraint Preliminary Credit Check Gather Additional Documents if A occurs B must occur after

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: PRECEDENCE constraint Quality Assurance Review Authorize Contract Terms Authorize cannot occur without prior QA Review

Name: Deductive Reasoning - NE
Text: CHAIN SUCCESSION constraint Quality Assurance Review Assemble Loan Offer Package immediately followed by

Name: Deductive Reasoning - NE
Text: CHAIN RESPONSE constraint Assemble Loan Offer Package Transfer Funds immediately followed by

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: COEXISTENCE constraint Gather Additional Documents Authorize Contract Terms both occur or neither

Name: Deductive Reasoning - NE
Text: ALTRESPONSE constraint Transfer Funds Notify Customer alternative at least one occurs

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: ALTPRECEDENCE constraint Notify Customer Transfer Funds alternative precedence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Ill analyze the remaining constraints that add complexity to the loan approval workflow

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The alternative succession between document gathering and quality assurance review provides flexibility while the non existence constraint prevents overlapping fund transfers and application receipt

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Non succession and non chain succession constraints further restrict certain activity sequences ensuring a structured and controlled process flow

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: These constraints essentially create a dynamic yet controlled sequence of loan processing activities allowing for some variation while maintaining strict operational boundaries
